rationale: Career is entirely non-technical (sales, marketing, accounting, operations) at two fictional companies (Wayne Enterprises, Stark Industries) with zero ML/IR engineering experience despite strong education credentials.
Marketing Manager with career in brand design and customer support at fictional companies (Initech), no engineering or ML experience whatsoever.

Operations Manager whose entire career is in logistics ops, customer support, and graphic design at two fictional companies (Dunder Mifflin, Pied Piper), with no ML engineering depth despite a Ph.D in Data Science.

Software engineer with PyTorch (advanced) and IIIT Bangalore education, but career is primarily IT-services (Cognizant) doing DevOps and frontend with only self-learner AI exposure and no production IR or retrieval experience.

3.8-year ML engineer at product companies (Glance, Razorpay) with advanced BM25 and Hugging Face Transformers skills, but career work is time-series forecasting rather than production IR/ranking systems, and total experience falls short of the 5-9 year target.

7.2-year data/backend engineer with advanced Information Retrieval (47 months) and Fine-tuning LLMs skills, but career narrative centers on data pipelines and warehouse engineering rather than production embedding retrieval or ranking, and two major stints are at IT-services firms (Wipro, Accenture).
Only 1.8 years of experience as a Java/mobile developer with no ML or IR background, just self-learner AI interest and skills in Spark and Rust unrelated to the role.
Sales Executive with career in content writing and SEO, no engineering or ML experience, and only 2.3 years of experience in entirely non-technical roles.
Project Manager at fictional Wayne Enterprises with 1.3 years of enterprise sales experience and no technical or ML engineering background.

10-year software engineer whose entire career spans IT-services/consulting firms (Infosys, Accenture) doing QA, full-stack, and DevOps, with advanced ASR skill but no IR/ranking or production ML system experience.
